title: centaurea mengenensis
intro: Centaurea mengenensis, also known as White Cockle or Mengen Chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and saponin content. The health benefits of Centaurea mengenensis include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional herbal medicine. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in the production of dried extracts and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
